raw_input:
[4.0/5] works great for haircuts I really needed this for my boys, I cut their hair and they always have hair all through their clothes and even though I tell them to shake there shirts right there when they stand up, there is always a trail of hair going down the hall to the bathroom. This is so nice, the neck is stretchy and fits different sizes of necks, easy to clasp shut and open. I only wish the back was longer like the front but I had no problem with keeping hair off them. It was really nice to only have to clean up in one small area. [4.0/5] Does the job Works well. Full coverage and easy to use closure- hook and eye on stretchy elastic. I don't know about it being "ultra soft", it feels like most hair cutting capes...kind of plastic like. Works for my family's needs. [2.0/5] Strange cut The front of this cape is super long (almost too long) however the back is cut in a way such that no matter how you adjust the neck, only about the top 12" of the back are covered, leaving the lower back exposed. It is like a long rectangle with the corners of the short end joined behind the neck at a diagonal. It is better than nothing but the cut is just off. Maybe if you have an actual barbers chair it works better. I was using a towel as a cape when I cut my husband and kids hair, and this on top of a towel for the lower back (or two of these capes alternating directions so the long part can go in the front and the back) should do a better job than just the towel. The fabric is cheap thin polyester, which gets the job done but is definitely not "ultra soft silk fiber" as stated in the description. The neck is easy to adjust and fasten, so at least it has that going for it. [5.0/5] Nice lightweight cape Worked great... adjustable clips make it perfect for any size person. The lightweight fabric gave me the protection I needed with the ability to move my arms around while weaving my hair. Also needed to let the hair product dry so sat outside in the sun for 30 minutes for the developer to dry. Was very glad that it wasn’t heavy material.

:) Recommend if you need to “Do It Yourself Hair Coloring” during the Covid crisis. [3.0/5] Safe For Dry Cutting It's nice size and good for our purpose, cutting hair.
And since it called a 'Barber's Cape' and thusly one would expect dry cutting
I'm not sure how relevant this is.

HOWEVER - it's also stated it's resistant to chemicals.
But I don't see how it could be protective to ones clothing when it's not water proof,
liquid pours right through, so it's not good for various processes using chemicals etc.
And the reason for 3 stars

The material around the neck is pleated / drawn together with elastic.
It's adjustable with metal hooks, but feels poky and uncomfortable. [4.0/5] Wish it was longer on the back My first impression is that it is very similar to what my barber has. But on a closer look, I found that the material is very thin. Also I really would like to have it longer on the back. When I cut hair on the back of the head, I wish the cape would be longer enough to cover the clothes. Otherwise, it defeats the purpose.

It has elastic ring around the neck with a metal clip. It works well. Price is very good. But you get what you pay for. [4.0/5] Good only for home use Since COVID-19 I have been cutting my mother's hair. I had been hoping to find a cape that I could protect her clothing while doing the cutting. I was very happy to be offered the opportunity to review this one.

The cape, in general, is okay. This is definitely not a professional quality cape that you find at salons. It's very thin and not waterproof. For dry cutting, it's perfect. This should not be considered for anything involving hair coloring.

The cape is quite large. It definitely serves the purpose of covering the entire body. It also has a nice, silky feel to it.

The fastening clasp is ultra-cheap and I found this to be the most unlikable aspect of this product. The clasps take considerable time to fiddle around with and fasten. It would have been a much better idea to offer velcro or button straps.

But here's the thing: This doesn't cost a lot of money. You definitely get what you pay for. So, if you are looking for something inexpensive for home cutting during the pandemic then this will fit the bill perfectly. [3.0/5] A Necessity for Home Hair Cutting Back in the golden oldie days B.C. (Before COVID-19), I wouldn’t have known the difference between a barber’s cape and a bullfighter’s cape. But today, home hair cutting is now the norm, and a barber’s cape is practically a necessity.

This SmoTecQ one does the job with little drama. Measuring an adult-sized 57 by 63 inches, it has an elasticized neckband with two thin but sturdy metal clasps that can adjust to fit tightly around any size neck. The fabric in the back tended to gape open a little when I tested it, and it’s much shorter in the back than in the front, but overall it does a good job at what it’s intended to do. Hair doesn’t stick to it too badly, so it’s relatively easy to wipe clean after use. Folding it up to try to put it neatly back into its plastic storage bag was a challenge.

That’s not to say that hair still doesn’t go all over the floor, though, and that’s why my amateur haircuts, whether I’m getting them or giving them, take place on one of my outdoor patios.

Based on my zero knowledge of what makes a good one or a bad one, I have to rate this barber’s cape at a middle-of-the-road three stars. I wish I didn’t need one at all, but in the current reality, it’s a must-have item, and this one seems to do the job. [4.0/5] Made of quality fabric. Must have for hair cutting. This barber’s haircutting cape is sufficiently large for any individual. It is made of good quality fabric. The hairs do not get stuck in it. I feel that the mechanism to tie it around the neck could be improved.

Other than that, it is a handy cape. [4.0/5] Nice barbers cape Nice long barbers cape. Feels like those at the barber’s/salon. No complaints [5.0/5] Important tool to have for haircuts Works as advertised. As someone who cut another person’s hair without this and got hair everywhere including the fabric of the chair the person was sitting in... I can tell you this is worth having. If you’re a newly minted quarantine hairstylist, get yourself one of these so you don’t get hair all over the person’s neck and back, clothes, etc. [5.0/5] great cape for hair cutting! perfect to cover your client while doing their hair. the closure is super nice too. [2.0/5] Let's Hair In I am not a professional, but I do give my boys haircuts at home. They hate getting hair all over them (and since there are 4 of them and I don't plan on stopping the home haircuts anytime soon) I thought it would be worth ordering a cape. I got an adult cape because my 10 yr old is almost as big as me and I know my other boys won't be far behind. This cape is terrible! Hair gets down the top of it and they're just as itchy as if they weren't wearing anything. The quality of the cape itself seems fine, but that doesn't really matter if it doesn't work for the purpose it was made for. [5.0/5] Works great for at-home haircuts I think many of us are attempting to stay groomed at home during the Covid 19 pandemic including myself. Using a plastic bag around my body was no longer cutting it. So I got this barber cape to use for my 5 feet 10 inch body and it's been working well so far. It's much more comfortable around my neck. There's no edges that tickle me like crazy. The sheer material used doesn't attract much hair and can be blown off easily. It also covers my body way better than my old plastic garbage bag did. [5.0/5] Thin and functional. Batman would be jealous. This cape works equally as well as a hair cutting cape as it does as a costume! I (jokingly) ran across the house with this as a cape when it arrived, and my son immediately commandeered it as his own.

In all seriousness, this works quite well as a hair cutting cape and keeps the clippings from landing all over you when you are getting your hair cut. My wife typically cuts my hair wet, and I did notice a bit of moisture coming through this, but not enough to bother me. If you are looking for a salon caliber cape, plan on spending twice as much. At this price, this works great for my needs. [5.0/5] Works well for its intended use! Love the feel of this item very soft!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers find this barber cape to be a decent, affordable option for home haircuts, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic. Most reviewers give it 4 out of 5 stars, noting that it does its basic job of keeping hair off clothing during dry cutting. The adjustable elastic neckband with metal clasps is generally well-received for fitting different neck sizes, though some find the clasps uncomfortable or fiddly to use.

The most common complaint is that the cape is significantly shorter in the back than the front, leaving the lower back exposed during haircuts. Several reviewers also note that the fabric is thin and not waterproof, making it unsuitable for hair coloring or chemical treatments, despite the product description claiming it is chemical-resistant and made of ultra-soft silk fiber. Reviewers largely agree the fabric description is misleading.

On the positive side, customers appreciate that hair does not stick to the material and is easy to wipe off. The cape is large enough to cover most adults, and the lightweight fabric is comfortable to wear. Multiple reviewers mention it is a good value for the price, as long as expectations are kept reasonable.

The cape is best suited for simple, dry home haircuts and is not recommended as a professional-grade product. Several reviewers specifically mention purchasing it during the pandemic as a practical, budget-friendly solution for at-home grooming.
